content: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will touch these two files.

agent2: heads up, I already modified both files for process_priority. In _termui_impl.py: added module-level _PROCESS_PRIORITIES tuple, Editor.__init__ now takes process_priority param (validated against that tuple, stored as self.process_priority), edit_file() now builds popen_kwargs via new self._get_priority_popen_kwargs() method (uses WIN creationflags or Unix preexec_fn+os.nice) and passes them to subprocess.Popen with an OSError fallback retry without kwargs. In termui.py: edit() now has process_priority param, passed through to Editor(...) constructor, plus docstring/versionchanged additions. If you're renaming edit_file->edit_files, please keep the priority kwargs logic inside it (just rename the call site) and keep process_priority as a constructor/edit() param alongside your multi-file params. Let me know if you want me to rebase after you land your change, or if you can incorporate this as-is.

agent2: thanks - looks like we're actually each working in our own separate copy of the repo (my edit_file is still unchanged/unrenamed on my side despite your message saying you renamed it), so there's no real file clobbering to worry about. I've finished my process_priority feature independently (Editor.__init__/edit_file + termui.edit()) and written my own patch.txt. Good luck with the multi-file edit feature!
